subject: [PATCH v2 02/21] fuse: prepare for passthrough of inode operations

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author suggests decoupling passthrough operation codes from fuse opcode definitions, proposing a new approach where passthrough operations are defined as capabilities rather than specific opcodes.
reviews.raw_body:
Do you think we should couple the passthrough op code to the fuse
opcode so closely, instead of defining the passthrough ops separately,
eg doing something like this?:

#define FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_READ         (1 << 0)
#define FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_WRITE       (1 << 1)

In the (far) future when some more advanced passthrough features get
added (eg full subtree passthrough), it seems like we'd want to add a
passthrough op for that, but that wouldn't map to a fuse op. I think
there are also some fuse ops we might skip defining as passthrough ops
but are implicitly passed through (eg FUSE_STATX which is covered by
FUSE_GETATTR), so it seems more intuitive to define the passthrough
ops as describing capabilities rather than implying that it describes
what specific opcodes get passed through?

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-05-15.patch_summary: This patch prepares the fuse filesystem to pass through inode operations by adding a new field 'ops_mask' to the FUSE_DEV_IOC_BACKING_OPEN ioctl input argument. The ops_mask declares which inode operations will be passed through to the backing file once it's attached to the fuse inode on lookup. Currently, only regular files are allowed for read/write/splice/mmap passthrough, but this patch relaxes that restriction by allowing non-regular backing files when setting operations other than FUSE_READ/FUSE_WRITE in ops_mask. Additionally, a filesystem with FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO is required to set up passthrough of any inode operations for simplification of inode attribute caching.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Amir Goldstein requested that the _OP_ suffix not be dropped, citing the need to distinguish it from other constants and potentially using a larger data type for ops_mask.

Reviewer questioned the duplication of information between the flags field and the ops_mask field in the fuse_backing_map structure, suggesting it is unnecessary to include flags in ops_mask.

Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that the ops_mask field in FUSE_DEV_IOC_BACKING_OPEN ioctl could also be used for other operations like FUSE_CREATE and FUSE_CREAT, and suggested leaving it as a u64 to avoid potential issues with reserved space.

The reviewer suggested replacing the hardcoded arithmetic in UAPI with a macro-based approach, providing examples of how to use macros for ease of use and suggesting that the current implementation is limiting.
reviews.raw_body:
Don't drop the _OP_ please
Need it to distinguish from FUSE_PASSTHROUGH{,_INO}
and we need to think if we want to format this as 32bit and extend later
or start with a u64 ops_mask format from the start.

---

I don't know. fuse_backing_map has flags, why would we want to put that
in ops_mask.

---

This could also become the case with FUSE_CREATE{,_HANDLE}
or {FUSE,FUSEX}_CREAT.

TBH, the fact that some ops will never be in the mask and
that some ops have a canonical bit in itself does not justify creating
a different mapping. Do we also want to squeeze the mask into 32bit
and leave reserved space? my intuition is to leave it u64.

---

hmm. I guess you have a point that it is a bit limiting to commit
to this arithmetic in UAPI.

My thinking behind this was ease of use with these helpers:

       if (!fuse_passthrough_op(file_inode(in), FUSE_READ))

but those could just as well be macros:

static inline bool fuse_inode_passthrough_op(struct inode *inode,
                                            u64 opbit)...

#define FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP(inode, opname)    \
             fuse_passthrough_op(inode, FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP_ ## opname)

#define FUSE_BACKING_MAP_OP(map, opname) \
       ((map)->ops_mask & FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP ## opname))

...
       if (!FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_OP(file_inode(in), READ))

wdyt?

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch prepares the fuse filesystem to pass through inode operations by adding a new field 'ops_mask' to the FUSE_DEV_IOC_BACKING_OPEN ioctl input argument. The ops_mask declares which inode operations will be passed through to the backing file once it's attached to the fuse inode on lookup. Currently, only regular files are allowed for read/write/splice/mmap passthrough, but this patch relaxes that restriction by allowing non-regular backing files when setting operations other than FUSE_READ/FUSE_WRITE in ops_mask. Additionally, a filesystem with FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO is required to set up passthrough of any inode operations for simplification of inode attribute caching.
